title: Add backend cart merge acceptance suite
verify: Real Medusa/PostgreSQL covers transfer, deterministic existing-target merge, and same-variant summing.
Authenticated evidence covers foreign ownership, incompatible carts, stock conflict, replay, and concurrency.
Existing-target success soft-deletes the source, ordinary Store CRUD returns not found, and journal-first replay returns the target.
Injected post-soft-delete failure restores source and target pre-merge state.
T3 closure includes /verify PASS, per-task semantic-pass, human checkpoint, and rollback/recovery evidence.

verify.summary: Manual T3 functional verification passed. The required packet was ready and hash-matched before verification; linked FT-003 API/security/data/state/testing specs were read; fresh backend cart-merge acceptance, backend typecheck, Memory Bank lint, and strict doctor gates passed. The backend suite executes against the Medusa route/workflow/module/PostgreSQL boundary and verifies transfer, deterministic existing-target merge, same-variant summing, foreign ownership denial, stock conflict no-mutation, journal-first replay without duplicate quantity, other-customer replay denial, in-progress concurrency response, consumed-source ordinary Store not-found behavior, and injected post-soft-delete source/target restoration. Task status remains unchanged; T3 closure still requires per-task /red-verify semantic-pass, HUMAN_CHECKPOINT: done, and ROLLBACK_RECOVERY_NOTE: present.

Per-task T3 adversarial semantic verification passed. TASK-025 solves the intended backend acceptance problem without production merge behavior changes, live providers, production data, browser UI scope, or hidden responsibility drift. The suite uses synthetic data over the Medusa route/workflow/module/PostgreSQL boundary and substantively covers ownership denial, deterministic merge, exact same-variant summing, replay without duplication, consumed-source not-found behavior, stock no-mutation, pending-journal in-progress response, and source/target restoration after injected failure. Residual route-level CRUD and high-load race assumptions are non-blocking for this task scope. T3 closure still requires explicit human checkpoint and rollback/recovery marker evidence before status can be done.

verify.closure_decision: Manual T3 closure explicitly requested by the user after /verify PASS and per-task /red-verify semantic-pass.
verify.summary: GENERAL closed TASK-025 in manual mode after explicit user approval. Required T3 gates are satisfied: full protocol evidence, required packet/spec gates, /verify PASS, per-task /red-verify semantic-pass, HUMAN_CHECKPOINT: done, and ROLLBACK_RECOVERY_NOTE: present. FT-003 and REQ-008 remain planned because downstream TASK-026 and feature-level semantic verification are still not closed.

evidence_required: Replay/concurrency and consumed-source Store not-found logs.
T3 human checkpoint and rollback/recovery note before closure.
constraints: Use synthetic local customers, carts, variants, and the real Medusa/PostgreSQL boundary.
Do not use live OAuth providers, production secrets, or production data.
Keep browser acceptance outside this task.
invariants: Browser mocks cannot prove ownership, persistence, replay, or compensation.
Consumed source is unavailable through Store CRUD but replayable through its journal.
No successful case may lose or duplicate quantity.
purpose: Provide independent server-side acceptance evidence for the complete data and security boundary.
success_outcome: REQ-008 server behavior is reproducibly proven against real Medusa/PostgreSQL, including consumed-source and recovery semantics.
anti_goals: Do not change production merge behavior to make tests pass.
Do not use live providers or production data.
Do not add browser UI coverage.

stop_conditions: Ownership or replay cannot be proven independently of browser UI.
Failure injection cannot prove source and target restoration.
